mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.94% suggests potential easing cycle if rate declines
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) over a 2-month window indicates labor market strength and robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained employment growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive PAYEMS signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 380230.0 million dollars, HOUST at 1331.0 thousand units
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST decline signal, or interaction effect (joint decline) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) versus single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices earnings downward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding a threshold of 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%) signals monetary policy tightness that constrains equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint tightness signal, or interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) versus single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows sustained expansion above 2.0% annualized rate, indicating robust economic fundamentals that support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through higher corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11520.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% combined with a simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals a demand-side shock that triggers negative equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1331.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 380230.0 million dollars
regime_hint: sharp demand contraction or demand-side shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite joint demand shock signal, or interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) versus single-series effects.
